The plant is known to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its potential in treating various health issues, such as wound healing, skin problems, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Anthericum nemorale include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Anthericum is commonly used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, often employed to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, as well as to aid in the recovery process after surgery or injury.
